input: More than mine own desert could justly claim, yet still thou dost to do for me
target: To do more for me than mine own desert,

input: For me to do more than what mine own desert alone could ever deserve or warrant
target: To do more for me than mine own desert,

input: And thou shalt hang more praise upon the deceased I, that noble soul departed hence.
target: And hang more praise upon deceased I

input: More praise shall hang upon the deceased I, and thus do we honour those who are no more.
target: And hang more praise upon deceased I

input: Hang more praise, I say, upon the deceased, and let it rest there evermore in testament to what was.
target: And hang more praise upon deceased I

input: Than what a niggard truth would willingly impart unto thee.
target: Than niggard truth would willingly impart:

input: More generous far than truth, that niggard, would willingly impart.
target: Than niggard truth would willingly impart:

input: Than truth, being niggard in nature, would willingly deign to impart.
target: Than niggard truth would willingly impart:

input: And so should you likewise be mov'd to love things of nothing worth, those empty vanities.
target: And so should you, to love things nothing worth.

input: To love things nothing worth—thus should you do the same, and so conduct yourself in such affection.
target: And so should you, to love things nothing worth.

input: And so too should you be inclin'd to love those things which are nothing worth, I say.
target: And so should you, to love things nothing worth.

input: In that time of year, thou mayst behold in me the marks of aging, as autumn doth display its fading glory.
target: That time of year thou mayst in me behold

input: Behold in me, I prithee, that time of year which thou mayst perceive, when all things wane as doth the dying season.
target: That time of year thou mayst in me behold

input: That time of year which thou art able to behold in me doth now arrive, and in my visage mayst thou see the very season itself.
target: That time of year thou mayst in me behold

input: Upon those boughs which do shake most violently against the bitter cold, there hangs a melancholy air.
target: Upon those boughs which shake against the cold,

input: Those boughs which shake and tremble against the piercing cold do rest upon the very earth, forsaken.
target: Upon those boughs which shake against the cold,

input: Against the cold, those boughs which shake do bend upon the frozen ground in their distress.
target: Upon those boughs which shake against the cold,

input: In me, good soul, thou seeest the twilight of such day as is now fading into darkness most profound.
target: In me thou seeest the twilight of such day

input: Thou seeest in me, I say, the twilight of such day—that melancholy hour when light doth flee.
target: In me thou seeest the twilight of such day

input: Behold in me the twilight of such day, which thou dost surely seeest with thine own eyes.
target: In me thou seeest the twilight of such day

input: Without all bail shall carry me away into that dark abyss from which no man returneth.
target: Without all bail shall carry me away,

input: Away shall carry me, without all bail to stay my passage, into the depths unknown.
target: Without all bail shall carry me away,

input: All bail being absent, away shall carry me without remedy or hope of return.
target: Without all bail shall carry me away,

input: Now art thou proud as an enjoyer, and anon thou shalt be humbled.
target: Now proud as an enjoyer, and anon

input: Proud as an enjoyer thou art now, and yet anon thy fortune shall turn.
target: Now proud as an enjoyer, and anon

input: Now, proud as one who is an enjoyer, and anon thy pride shall fade away.
target: Now proud as an enjoyer, and anon

input: Then bettered it, that the world may see my pleasure and know my will manifest.
target: Then bettered that the world may see my pleasure:

input: That the world may see my pleasure, then bettered I the state of things.
target: Then bettered that the world may see my pleasure:

input: Then bettered was that which the world may see of my pleasure and contentment.
target: Then bettered that the world may see my pleasure:

input: And by and by, being clean starved for a look, thou dost languish most pitifully.
target: And by and by clean starved for a look;

input: By and by, methinks, clean starved for a look shalt thou become, and waste away.
target: And by and by clean starved for a look;

input: And by and by, when thou art clean starved for a look from thy beloved, despair doth overtake thee.
target: And by and by clean starved for a look;

input: Art thou so far removed from all variation, or any quick change of fortune's wheel?
target: So far from variation or quick change?

input: Dost thou remain so far from variation, or from any quick change that might befall thee?
target: So far from variation or quick change?

input: How canst thou be so far from variation, or from quick change, when all the world doth shift?
target: So far from variation or quick change?

input: Why doth it come to pass that I do write still, all one and ever the same, without variation or change?
target: Why write I still all one, ever the same,

input: Ever the same do I remain, and why write I still, all one, in this most constant and unvarying manner?
target: Why write I still all one, ever the same,

input: Tell me, why write I still in all one fashion, ever the same, with no departure from this settled course?
target: Why write I still all one, ever the same,

input: That every word doth almost tell my name, as though each utterance were a mirror held unto my very soul.
target: That every word doth almost tell my name,

input: Every word that passeth from thy lips doth almost tell my name, so near the truth art thou in speech.
target: That every word doth almost tell my name,

input: That every word which doth issue forth almost tell my name, betraying thus the secrets of my heart.
target: That every word doth almost tell my name,

input: How doth thy dial show thee that thy precious minutes waste away?
target: Thy dial how thy precious minutes waste;

input: Thy dial doth reveal, dear friend, how thy precious minutes waste themselves in time's swift passage.
target: Thy dial how thy precious minutes waste;

input: Behold thy dial, and observe how thy precious minutes waste and slip from thy very grasp.
target: Thy dial how thy precious minutes waste;

input: So oft have I invoked thee, crying out in desperate supplication for my Muse, that thou might'st grant me inspiration.
target: So oft have I invoked thee for my Muse,

input: I have so oft invoked thee for my Muse, calling upon thy divine grace with such frequency that the heavens themselves must tire of my pleas.
target: So oft have I invoked thee for my Muse,

input: For my Muse have I so oft invoked thee in prayer, beseeching thy favour with a constancy that doth speak of my deepest need.
target: So oft have I invoked thee for my Muse,

input: As every alien pen hath got my use, so too doth it employ me in its foreign service.
target: As every alien pen hath got my use

input: For every alien pen that hath got my use doth wield it as its own instrument.
target: As every alien pen hath got my use

input: Inasmuch as every alien pen hath got my use, it taketh what was mine and maketh it serve its purposes.
target: As every alien pen hath got my use

input: And with heavy ignorance doth one seek aloft to fly.
target: And heavy ignorance aloft to fly,

input: Heavy ignorance, and yet aloft to fly it doth aspire.
target: And heavy ignorance aloft to fly,

input: To fly aloft with heavy ignorance, and thus to soar.
target: And heavy ignorance aloft to fly,

input: Yet be thou most proud of that which I do compile with mine own hand.
target: Yet be most proud of that which I compile,

input: Be most proud, I say, of that which I compile, and yet let thy pride be steadfast.
target: Yet be most proud of that which I compile,

input: Yet thou shalt be most proud of that very thing which I compile through my labours.
target: Yet be most proud of that which I compile,

input: And let the arts be graced with thy sweet graces, which do adorn them most beautifully.
target: And arts with thy sweet graces graced be;

input: And arts, I say, shall be most graced with thy sweet graces, which are bestowed upon them.
target: And arts with thy sweet graces graced be;

input: And with thy sweet graces let the arts be graced, for thou dost possess such noble qualities.
target: And arts with thy sweet graces graced be;

input: And my most sick Muse doth give unto an other place her troubled song.
target: And my sick Muse doth give an other place.

input: My sick Muse, I say, doth give an other place unto her weary voice, and there remaineth.
target: And my sick Muse doth give an other place.

input: And doth my sick Muse give an other place, forsaking all her former seat.
target: And my sick Muse doth give an other place.

input: I do grant unto thee, sweet love, that thy argument is most lovely and doth move my heart.
target: I grant, sweet love, thy lovely argument

input: Sweet love, I grant thee this: thy lovely argument hath persuaded me most thoroughly.
target: I grant, sweet love, thy lovely argument

input: Thy lovely argument, sweet love, I freely grant unto thee with all my affection.
target: I grant, sweet love, thy lovely argument

input: Then thank him not for that which he doth say, for his words may deceive thine ear.
target: Then thank him not for that which he doth say,

input: Then thank not him for that which doth he say, since gratitude ill-befits such utterance.
target: Then thank him not for that which he doth say,

input: Thank him not then for that which he doth say, for the words he speaks deserve no thanks.
target: Then thank him not for that which he doth say,

input: O, how I doth faint when I do write of you!
target: O how I faint when I of you do write,

input: O, when I write of you, how greatly do I faint!
target: O how I faint when I of you do write,

input: O, how I faint away when of you I do write!
target: O how I faint when I of you do write,

input: To make me tongue-tied, speaking of your fame!
target: To make me tongue-tied speaking of your fame!

input: Speaking of your fame doth make me tongue-tied, to such degree!
target: To make me tongue-tied speaking of your fame!

input: The speaking of your fame would make me tongue-tied, to render me thus!
target: To make me tongue-tied speaking of your fame!

input: The humble doth bear as the proudest sail, in measure equal and undiminished.
target: The humble as the proudest sail doth bear,

input: As the proudest sail doth bear its burden vast, so too the humble doth bear with noble grace.
target: The humble as the proudest sail doth bear,

input: The humble bear their load as steadfast as the proudest sail doth bear upon the water.
target: The humble as the proudest sail doth bear,

input: My bark, which is saucy in nature yet inferior far to his own vessel, doth sail the seas with lesser might.
target: My saucy bark, inferior far to his,

input: Inferior far to his most noble bark am I, with my saucy craft that beareth not the grandeur of his ship.
target: My saucy bark, inferior far to his,

input: My saucy bark, though it be inferior far when compared to the majesty of his, remaineth yet a worthy vessel.
target: My saucy bark, inferior far to his,

input: He who doth stand of tall building, and whose nature is of goodly pride most manifest.
target: He of tall building, and of goodly pride:

input: A man of tall building is he, and of goodly pride doth he partake in full measure.
target: He of tall building, and of goodly pride:

input: He beareth the qualities of tall building, and of goodly pride doth his countenance shine forth.
target: He of tall building, and of goodly pride:

input: The worst of all my sorrows was this most lamentable truth: my love, that which I cherished above all else, did prove to be my own decay and ruin.
target: The worst was this: my love was my decay.

input: This, I confess, was the very worst that could befall me—that my love itself became the instrument of my decay, bringing me to destruction.
target: The worst was this: my love was my decay.

input: Nay, the worst calamity that ever befell me was this cruel circumstance: my love, which should have been my greatest joy, was instead my decay and undoing.
target: The worst was this: my love was my decay.

input: Though I, once gone from this mortal realm, must die to all the world and be remembered no more;
target: Though I, once gone, to all the world must die:

input: Though I must die to all the world once I am gone, yet shall my memory persist;
target: Though I, once gone, to all the world must die:

input: Though to all the world I must surely die once I am gone from hence;
target: Though I, once gone, to all the world must die:

input: Which eyes, not yet created in this world, shall over-read and comprehend with great labour;
target: Which eyes not yet created shall over-read;

input: Eyes not yet created shall, in time to come, over-read that which is written here;
target: Which eyes not yet created shall over-read;

input: That which eyes not yet created shall hereafter over-read with diligent study and care;
target: Which eyes not yet created shall over-read;

input: I do grant unto thee that thou wert not married to my Muse, neither by bond nor by sacred vow.
target: I grant thou wert not married to my Muse,

input: Grant I that thou wert never married unto my Muse, for such a union was not meant to be.
target: I grant thou wert not married to my Muse,

input: I grant, in truth, that thou wert not married to my Muse, though it might have been so in another age.
target: I grant thou wert not married to my Muse,

input: And therefore mayst thou, without attaint, overlook that which troubleth thee
target: And therefore mayst without attaint overlook

input: Without attaint, therefore mayst thou overlook and pass by judgment upon it
target: And therefore mayst without attaint overlook

input: And so therefore, without blemish or attaint upon thy soul, mayst thou overlook the matter
target: And therefore mayst without attaint overlook

input: Thou art as fair in hue as thou dost prove thyself in knowledge, gentle soul.
target: Thou art as fair in knowledge as in hue,

input: In knowledge, as in hue, art thou most fair and beauteous to behold.
target: Thou art as fair in knowledge as in hue,

input: As fair in hue dost thou appear, so too art thou in knowledge equally fair and bright.
target: Thou art as fair in knowledge as in hue,

input: And therefore art thou enforced to seek anew that which was lost before.
target: And therefore art enforced to seek anew

input: And therefore thou art most grievously enforced to seek anew thy fortunes.
target: And therefore art enforced to seek anew

input: And therefore, being enforced by circumstance, art thou constrained to seek anew.
target: And therefore art enforced to seek anew

input: And do so, I prithee, love; yet when they shall have devised their schemes, mark well what comes to pass.
target: And do so, love; yet when they have devised,

input: Do so, I beseech thee, love; and yet when they have devised and plotted their course, consider what shall follow.
target: And do so, love; yet when they have devised,

input: And do so, my love, I say; yet mark me well—when they have devised their plans, what then shall come to thee?
target: And do so, love; yet when they have devised,

input: Thou, being truly fair, wert truly sympathized withal by all who didst behold thy countenance.
target: Thou truly fair, wert truly sympathized

input: Truly wert thou fair, and truly sympathized with by those whose hearts were moved to compassion.
target: Thou truly fair, wert truly sympathized

input: Being truly fair, thou wert truly sympathized by all men, for thy beauty didst stir their deepest sympathies.
target: Thou truly fair, wert truly sympathized

input: And their gross painting might be better used in service to some worthier purpose.
target: And their gross painting might be better used

input: Their gross painting, and the craft thereof, might be better used than it is presently employed.
target: And their gross painting might be better used

input: And if their gross painting might be better used, then wherefore doth it serve such base ends?
target: And their gross painting might be better used

input: How far doth a modern quill come too short of that which it ought to achieve!
target: How far a modern quill doth come too short,

input: A modern quill, alas, doth come too short—how very far it falleth from sufficiency!
target: How far a modern quill doth come too short,

input: Too short doth come a modern quill, and how far indeed from excellence it remaineth!
target: How far a modern quill doth come too short,

input: Than both your poets can in praise devise, there is no greater thing that they might speak.
target: Than both your poets can in praise devise.

input: More excellent than what both your poets can devise in their praise and utterance combined.
target: Than both your poets can in praise devise.

input: In praise they cannot devise aught greater than both your poets can, try though they might.
target: Than both your poets can in praise devise.

input: I do think good thoughts, whilst others do write good words in their stead.
target: I think good thoughts, whilst others write good words,

input: Whilst others write good words, I think good thoughts within my mind.
target: I think good thoughts, whilst others write good words,

input: Good thoughts occupy my mind, I think, whilst others write good words upon the page.
target: I think good thoughts, whilst others write good words,

input: To every hymn that able spirit doth affords, most gracefully and willingly
target: To every hymn that able spirit affords,

input: Every hymn to which that able spirit affords itself, thou shalt attend with reverence
target: To every hymn that able spirit affords,

input: To each and every hymn that any able spirit may affords unto thee with bounty
target: To every hymn that able spirit affords,

input: In the polished form of a well-refined pen doth the writer's art most eloquently appear.
target: In polished form of well-refined pen.

input: The pen, well-refined and polished in its form, doth produce words of exquisite refinement.
target: In polished form of well-refined pen.

input: Behold the well-refined pen in its polished form, wherein the craft of writing is most perfectly wrought.
target: In polished form of well-refined pen.

input: Then do others, for the breath of words, show respect
target: Then others, for the breath of words respect,

input: For the breath of words do others then hold respect
target: Then others, for the breath of words respect,

input: Then others hold respect, I say, for the very breath of words
target: Then others, for the breath of words respect,

input: Was it his spirit, which by spirits taught did learn to write?
target: Was it his spirit, by spirits taught to write,

input: By spirits taught to write, was it his spirit that did so accomplish?
target: Was it his spirit, by spirits taught to write,

input: His spirit, taught by spirits to write, was it not the instrument thereof?
target: Was it his spirit, by spirits taught to write,

input: My verse, in giving him aid, did leave all astonished.
target: Giving him aid, my verse astonished.

input: Giving him aid through my verse, I astonished all who beheld it.
target: Giving him aid, my verse astonished.

input: By giving him aid, my verse hath astonished the world most wonderfully.
target: Giving him aid, my verse astonished.

input: He, that affable and familiar ghost, doth trouble thee not, nor any soul besides.
target: He, nor that affable familiar ghost

input: Neither he nor that most affable familiar ghost, which doth appear so courteous and near, shall haunt thee more.
target: He, nor that affable familiar ghost

input: That affable familiar ghost, he being no more, nor shall it visit thee—nay, not that ghost so friendly in its aspect.
target: He, nor that affable familiar ghost

input: I was not sick of any fear that did proceed from thence, I say unto thee.
target: I was not sick of any fear from thence:

input: From thence there came no fear of which I was sick, not any fear whatsoever.
target: I was not sick of any fear from thence:

input: Not sick was I of any fear that issued forth from thence in those days.
target: I was not sick of any fear from thence:

input: Farewell unto thee, for thou art too dear a treasure for my possessing of thee.
target: Farewell! thou art too dear for my possessing,

input: Farewell, dear soul! Thou art far too precious and costly for my possessing, I say.
target: Farewell! thou art too dear for my possessing,

input: Farewell! For thou art grown too dear, and thus too dear for my poor possessing.
target: Farewell! thou art too dear for my possessing,

input: And like enough, thou knowest well thy estimate of thyself and thy true worth.
target: And like enough thou knowest thy estimate,

input: And thou dost know, like enough, what thy estimate truly is, for well thou knowest it.
target: And like enough thou knowest thy estimate,

input: Like enough it is that thou knowest thy estimate, and art acquainted with thy proper valuation.
target: And like enough thou knowest thy estimate,

input: Upon thy part, I can set down a story of such import as shall move thy heart.
target: Upon thy part I can set down a story

input: A story can I set down upon thy part, for I possess the skill to record thy noble deeds.
target: Upon thy part I can set down a story

input: I can set down upon thy part a story most worthy, that all the world may know thy virtue.
target: Upon thy part I can set down a story

input: In doing thee vantage, I beseech thee to afford me double-vantage in return, for what profiteth thy gain if I receive not twofold benefit?
target: Doing thee vantage, double-vantage me.

input: Thou receivest vantage through my actions, yet I must entreat thee: grant me double-vantage, that the doing may balance betwixt us both.
target: Doing thee vantage, double-vantage me.

input: If doing thee such vantage be my task, then surely thou shouldst recompense me with double-vantage, lest I be cheated of my rightful due.
target: Doing thee vantage, double-vantage me.

input: Say unto me that thou didst forsake me for some grievous fault of mine own.
target: Say that thou didst forsake me for some fault,

input: Prithee, say that for some fault thou didst forsake and abandon me.
target: Say that thou didst forsake me for some fault,

input: I beseech thee, say that thou didst for some fault forsake me entirely.
target: Say that thou didst forsake me for some fault,

input: Thou canst not love to disgrace me half so ill as I fear thou might'st.
target: Thou canst not love disgrace me half so ill,

input: Canst thou not love me, yet disgrace me half so ill? Nay, it is not possible.
target: Thou canst not love disgrace me half so ill,

input: That thou canst not disgrace me half so ill through love is a mercy I scarce deserve.
target: Thou canst not love disgrace me half so ill,

input: Thy sweet beloved name shall dwell no more within the hearts of men, nor find abode in mortal memory henceforth.
target: Thy sweet beloved name no more shall dwell,

input: No more shall thy beloved sweet name dwell in this world, for it must fade from all remembrance and be forgot.
target: Thy sweet beloved name no more shall dwell,

input: Thy name, most sweet and beloved, shall dwell in these lands no more, being cast into eternal oblivion from this day forth.
target: Thy sweet beloved name no more shall dwell,

input: Lest I, being too much given to profane utterance, should do it wrong thereby.
target: Lest I, too much profane, should do it wrong,

input: Lest I, who art too much profane in my speech, should do it wrong with mine own hand.
target: Lest I, too much profane, should do it wrong,

input: Lest I, too much corrupted in profane ways, should do it most grievously wrong.
target: Lest I, too much profane, should do it wrong,

input: For I must never love him whom thou dost so greatly hate, nor shall my heart betray thy wishes thus.
target: For I must never love him whom thou dost hate.

input: Verily, I must never love him whom thou dost hate, for such affection would be grievous betrayal of thy will.
target: For I must never love him whom thou dost hate.

input: For it is that I must never love him whom thou dost hate, having sworn mine eternal duty unto thee.
target: For I must never love him whom thou dost hate.

input: Then hate me, I prithee, when thou wilt it so; if ever such hatred shall come to pass, let it be now, this very moment.
target: Then hate me when thou wilt; if ever, now;

input: Hate me then, if it be thy pleasure so to do when thou wilt; and if such enmity must ever befall me, I beseech thee let it come upon me now.
target: Then hate me when thou wilt; if ever, now;

input: Then shall hate me when thou wilt, as is thy desire; if ever this bitter passion must touch my heart, let it be now, I implore thee.
target: Then hate me when thou wilt; if ever, now;

input: Join thy cruel forces with the spite of fortune, that they may make me bow in submission.
target: Join with the spite of fortune, make me bow,

input: Make me bow, I beseech thee, when thou dost join thy malice with the bitter spite of fortune.
target: Join with the spite of fortune, make me bow,

input: The spite of fortune shall join with thy dark will to make me bow and bend my stubborn knee.
target: Join with the spite of fortune, make me bow,

input: Ah! I do beseech thee, do not, when my heart hath at last 'scaped from this deep sorrow, bring new anguish upon me.
target: Ah! do not, when my heart hath 'scaped this sorrow,

input: Ah! do not, I pray thee, when my weary heart hath finally 'scaped the chains of this sorrow, wound it anew with fresh pain.
target: Ah! do not, when my heart hath 'scaped this sorrow,

input: Ah! do not, when my heart hath fortunately 'scaped away from this grievous sorrow, return to torment it further, I do implore thee.
target: Ah! do not, when my heart hath 'scaped this sorrow,

input: Give not unto a windy night a rainy morrow, I do beseech thee.
target: Give not a windy night a rainy morrow,

input: A windy night shall not be given a rainy morrow—give it not, I say.
target: Give not a windy night a rainy morrow,

input: Give not a rainy morrow to a windy night, lest sorrow compound upon sorrow.
target: Give not a windy night a rainy morrow,

input: When other petty griefs have done their spite upon thee, and all their malice hath been spent
target: When other petty griefs have done their spite,

input: When other griefs, though petty in their nature, have done their spite and exhausted all their bitter force
target: When other petty griefs have done their spite,

input: When all the other petty griefs have done their spite and finished with their vexing work complete
target: When other petty griefs have done their spite,

input: But come thou in the onset, so shall I taste of what doth follow.
target: But in the onset come: so shall I taste

input: In the onset come, I say, but so shall I taste thy measure.
target: But in the onset come: so shall I taste

input: But so shall I taste if in the onset thou come forth.
target: But in the onset come: so shall I taste

input: Some men do place their trust and pride in hawks and hounds, whilst others put their faith in horse.
target: Some in their hawks and hounds, some in their horse;

input: In hawks and hounds do some men find their joy, yet some there be who glory most in horse.
target: Some in their hawks and hounds, some in their horse;

input: Some doth invest their wealth in hawks and hounds, some in their horse do set their heart and store.
target: Some in their hawks and hounds, some in their horse;

input: And having thee in my possession, of all men's pride do I boast most loudly and with great spirit.
target: And having thee, of all men's pride I boast:

input: Of all men's pride, I boast exceedingly, and having thee doth make it so.
target: And having thee, of all men's pride I boast:

input: Having thee at my side, I boast of all men's pride as mine own greatest treasure and joy.
target: And having thee, of all men's pride I boast:

input: Wretched art thou in this one thing alone, that thou mayst take what thou dost desire.
target: Wretched in this alone, that thou mayst take

input: In this alone thou art most wretched, for that thou mayst take whatsoever thy heart doth covet.
target: Wretched in this alone, that thou mayst take

input: That thou mayst take doth make thee wretched in this alone, and in no other thing besides.
target: Wretched in this alone, that thou mayst take

input: All of this away doth thou carry, and me most wretched make thereby.
target: All this away, and me most wretched make.

input: Take all this away, I pray thee, and make me most wretched in thy departure.
target: All this away, and me most wretched make.

input: This all away shall go, and leave me most wretched, as thou dost make me so.
target: All this away, and me most wretched make.

input: Then need I not to fear the worst of wrongs, for I am fortified by virtue's shield.
target: Then need I not to fear the worst of wrongs,

input: Then do I not need to fear the worst of wrongs that might befall me in this mortal coil.
target: Then need I not to fear the worst of wrongs,

input: Then shall I not need to fear the worst of wrongs, being thus protected by fortune's grace.
target: Then need I not to fear the worst of wrongs,

input: When in the least of them my life hath come to its end, so shall I perish.
target: When in the least of them my life hath end.

input: In the least of them, when my life doth hath its end, there shall be my final breath.
target: When in the least of them my life hath end.

input: When my life hath reached its end in the least of them, so passes my mortal span.
target: When in the least of them my life hath end.

target: Thou usurer, that puttest forth all to use,

target: That she might think me some untutored youth,

input: And so doth the general of hot desire command thy soul with burning passion
target: And so the general of hot desire

input: And so the general, that commander of hot desire, doth reign o'er all thy faculties
target: And so the general of hot desire

input: And so there cometh the general of hot desire, that fierce captain of yearning flames
target: And so the general of hot desire

input: He was, whilst sleeping soundly, by a virgin hand most gently disarmed of all his weapons.
target: Was, sleeping, by a virgin hand disarmed.

input: Disarmed by a virgin hand, he was, even as he lay there sleeping in innocent repose.
target: Was, sleeping, by a virgin hand disarmed.

input: A virgin hand didst find him sleeping, and by her touch was he most cunningly disarmed of his arms.
target: Was, sleeping, by a virgin hand disarmed.
